title: The Ink That Doesn't Dry
body: Permit denials are stacking up when a sealed charter case lands on your desk.
The basin is running out of food, and the Fracture Choir pressure keeps getting worse.

choices.text: Take the charter straight to Synod council
Check Debt Gate market before reporting
Verify charter signatures in the archive first

title: Witness Line
body: Debt collectors hit your route, and Maela keeps you alive long enough to reach council.
Now you must decide who gets a legal voice in the ash charter hearing.

choices.text: Accept Maela as official escort
Refuse escort and file alone
Pause for River Commons testimony

title: Lawful Harm
body: Council asks you to sign a denial that leaves a workers' ward without aid.
Either way, people pay the price, and your choice decides how fast the ash crisis escalates.

choices.text: Hold the line and keep the process documented

success.text: You keep the convoy moving and preserve a legal record.

mixed.text: Aid moves forward, but your team takes smoke injuries.

failForward.text: The route collapses and forces a direct push to the weirs.

effects.message: Fail-forward: lawful process collapses into forced breach.

choices.text: Break protocol and force aid through
Leak the denial order to ward leaders

title: The Machine Under Drought
body: Under Hollow Weirs, you find the glass engine that has been rationing scarcity by design.
If you stall now, unrest spreads across both regions before sunrise.

choices.text: Breach now and expose the engine
Delay and ask for negotiated access
Capture evidence, then breach immediately

title: Charter of Ash
body: At the core, you must choose what replaces emergency rule.
Your signature will shape food access, law, and trust across the basin.

choices.text: Release ledgers and open water access
Centralize control under emergency command
Lock a three-faction compact into law

title: Dawn Ledger Release
body: The hidden ledgers go public, and relief routes open without private gate favors.
People call it the first real accord the basin has seen in years.

choices.text: Lock in the Dawn Ledger outcome

title: Regency of Ash
body: You keep order through one command chain and emergency authority.
Violence drops fast, but fear replaces public trust.

choices.text: Lock in the Ash Regency outcome

title: Floodline Compact
body: You force every faction into a shared charter with rotating witnesses.
The city stabilizes, but every clause creates new political debt.

choices.text: Lock in the Floodline Compact outcome

title: Rain Without Permission (Prelude)
body: The first famine loop breaks, but unrest spreads as people read the full receipts.
A new flashpoint grows in Mirror March, and you are pulled into it next.

choices.text: Follow the trail into Mirror March
Log this chapter and return to atlas

title: Riot of Receipts
body: Ledger leaks trigger protests from canal wards to tower courts.
If no accord lands soon, militias will try to take the gates by force.

choices.text: Trace receipts through Open Cistern records
Check Mirror Basin warnings first
Rally crews at Floodline Forge first

title: Terms of Mercy
body: Serin proposes a transparent gate lottery while Maela recruits crews still angry from past losses.
The wording of this compact decides who trusts the next enforcement step.

choices.text: Draft terms with strict legal language

success.text: The draft is clear enough for key wards to sign.

mixed.text: You get signatures, but rival houses start counter-campaigns.

failForward.text: Talks break down and force a rough shortcut.

choices.text: Draft terms from field testimony
Run an open ward vote on terms

title: Court of Smoke
body: Mirror Court offers fast passage if you pay with memory and market control.
Say yes and move fast, or refuse and risk open conflict near the weirs as the Choir rises.

choices.text: Pay memory collateral for passage
Refuse and cross under pressure
Leak monopoly terms to public councils

title: Weirfall
body: Storm surge and militia fire collide at Hollow Weirs as your coalition starts to crack.
This final descent decides whether the basin gets a shared accord or a new regent.

choices.text: Descend now and face the engine core
Wait for calmer channels
Evacuate civilians, then descend

title: The Regent Seat
body: At the regent seat, Cael offers controlled stability if you keep centralized power.
You can decentralize rights, enforce a veil, or bind everyone into one compact.

choices.text: Sign decentralized water rights
Keep controlled regency in place
Force one basin-wide compact

title: Rain Without Permission
body: The regent seat is cut from the engine, and neighborhoods form gate councils overnight.
Shared access and public records become the new default.

choices.text: Lock in Rain Without Permission

title: Veil Regency
body: You preserve emergency control and restrict movement through approved corridors only.
Street violence drops, but long-term resentment grows.

choices.text: Lock in the Veil Regency outcome

title: Charter Chorus Compact
body: Every faction signs one synchronized charter with common enforcement.
War pauses, but local autonomy shrinks under one legal rhythm.

choices.text: Lock in the Charter Chorus outcome

title: Basin Epilogue
body: Your final ruling stabilizes the basin, but every district still carries visible damage.
Outer envoys arrive with new glass oath scripts, hinting this fight is bigger than one city.

choices.text: Archive this run and return to atlas
Revisit Salt Gate aftermath reports

title: Cinder Market Briefing
body: Brokers auction route rumors and Choir telemetry while faction buyers posture around smoky ledgers.
Whatever you purchase here changes who reaches the next crisis front first.

choices.text: Shadow a broker into restricted stalls
Bid on encrypted crisis route data

success.text: You outbid rivals and secure verified route timings.

mixed.text: You win partial data and owe a dangerous favor.

failForward.text: You lose the bid, but steal enough clues while exiting.

choices.text: Withdraw before terms harden against you

title: Root Archive Vestibule
body: The living archive catalogs every prior Fracture Choir cycle and who profited from each collapse.
Your next move determines whether knowledge stays civic, military, or black-market.

choices.text: Descend for sealed crisis annals
Negotiate with the root curator
Close your folio and leave quietly

title: Mire Crossing Under Pressure
body: Acid fog rises as bridge crews rush supplies meant for Choir containment lines upstream.
Crossing decisions here decide whether civilians get aid before the next surge.

choices.text: Commit to the bridge and test anchors
Drop to the shrine for emergency stores

title: Mirror Lake Diagnostics
body: The basin reflects possible outcomes of your current policy choices.
Step in for hard foresight, or walk away and trust your faction judgment under crisis pressure.

choices.text: Enter the reflection to audit futures
Reject the vision and preserve present judgment

title: Shard Forge Mobilization
body: Crews are repairing tools needed to keep relief routes open this week.
Helping now affects who controls movement when pressure spikes again.

choices.text: Join the line and pay in blood
Negotiate terms and fund repairs with mana

title: Backroom Negotiation
body: Smugglers move heatproof gear and intercepted dispatches from both arc theaters in one room.
Your handling of this exchange sets trust and rumor flow for upcoming fronts.

choices.text: Purchase gear and lock in smuggler trust
Pressure the courier for route names

success.text: The courier breaks and names gate schedules in full.

mixed.text: You get fragments, but earn a lasting grudge.

failForward.text: Steel flashes, yet the satchel opens during the struggle.

choices.text: Disengage before leverage flips on you

title: Route Ledger Settlement
body: Final signatures decide whether evac lanes serve civilians first or private buyers first.
It looks like trade paperwork, but it is a direct power decision.

choices.text: Fund civilian caravans and stabilize routes
Sell route priority to the Undercourt

title: Deepstack Retrieval
body: Burned stacks hold records tying old regent offices to engine control chains.
Publishing them can support reform or feed propaganda.

choices.text: Study sealed annals despite spore risk

success.text: You reconstruct the lineage with precise archival control.

mixed.text: You secure truth, but leave with toxic lung scars.

failForward.text: Stacks collapse, yet one critical scroll survives in hand.

choices.text: Salvage practical tools for field teams

title: Living Index Decision
body: The index updates live as factions submit claims to legitimacy.
Who gets these records first will shape trust in the next ruling cycle.

choices.text: Release records to pilgrim councils
Sell curated pages to Concord command

title: Midspan Gale
body: Bridge cables are failing while workers move relief supply crates through acid fog.
You can push through, stabilize the route, or pull back and save people first.

choices.text: Sprint the span before the cables snap

success.text: You cross cleanly and mark a safe emergency lane.

mixed.text: You make it across, but inhale burning vapor.

failForward.text: You slip hard, recover, and finish the route bleeding.

choices.text: Stay and stabilize the bridge cables

success.text: You secure the line and keep aid moving.

mixed.text: The line holds, but you lose supplies in the fog.

failForward.text: You fail to hold the bridge but evacuate most workers.

choices.text: Pull back with survivors and regroup

title: Sunken Relief Cache
body: A sealed chest under the shrine holds rope kits and stimulant salts for bridge crews.
Take supplies for yourself or leave them for wider relief.

choices.text: Open the chest and claim emergency gear
Leave supplies and secure the route marker

title: Underface Current
body: Below the surface, alternate timelines argue over your prior rulings in Ember Hollow and the Steppe.
Accepting insight grants precision but increases psychological strain.

choices.text: Accept the painful forecast and remember it

success.text: You surface with a clear and actionable forecast.

mixed.text: You keep the omen, but it leaves a lasting migraine.

failForward.text: The vision fractures your confidence, yet reveals one key warning.

choices.text: Abort the dive and keep your judgment intact

title: Wake of Splintered Futures
body: Your reflection lags by a heartbeat while scouts ask whether to act on what you learned.
Sharing or hoarding the omen changes who trusts your crisis doctrine.

choices.text: Brief Concord scouts on the omen
Keep the omen private and equip quietly

title: Quench Line
body: The quench line can restore navigation tools if the timing stays exact under crisis pressure.
You can run precision duty, manage triage, or cut output to protect evacuation crews.

choices.text: Run precision hammer duty

success.text: Your strike cadence stabilizes the full production run.

mixed.text: The batch survives, but your hands blister badly.

failForward.text: A spill burns the floor, yet you salvage enough output.

choices.text: Shift to crew triage and safety control
Cut output and keep crews safe

title: Commission Routing
body: The repaired compass can guide pilgrim relief columns or privileged Undercourt convoys.
This dispatch determines who moves first when pressure spikes again.

choices.text: Route the compass to pilgrim caravans
Sell first-route rights to the Undercourt
